properties.name: Martin Luther King Jr. Center
properties.description: The official memorial dedicated to the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Contains his tomb and the Eternal Flame.

properties.name: Martin Luther King Jr. Birth Home
properties.description: The childhood home of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., a National Historic Site where he was born on January 15, 1929.

properties.name: Ebenezer Baptist Church
properties.description: Historic church where Dr. King was baptized and later served as co-pastor with his father. A cornerstone of the Civil Rights Movement.

properties.name: Ponce City Market
properties.description: Historic Sears building transformed into a mixed-use development featuring local restaurants, shops, and a rooftop amusement park.
properties.address: 675 Ponce De Leon Ave NE, Atlanta, GA 30308

properties.name: Krog Street Market
properties.description: Restored 1920s warehouse now housing artisanal food vendors, restaurants, and local retailers along the BeltLine.

properties.name: Dad's Garage Theatre
properties.description: Award-winning improv and theatre company known for irreverent comedy and community engagement since 1995.

properties.name: Fox Theatre
properties.description: Iconic 1929 Moorish-Egyptian revival movie palace, now a premier performing arts venue and Atlanta landmark.

properties.name: High Museum of Art
properties.description: The leading art museum in the Southeast, featuring renowned collections and Richard Meier's iconic white architecture.

properties.name: Zoo Atlanta
properties.description: One of the oldest zoos in the U.S., home to giant pandas and located in historic Grant Park since 1889.

properties.name: Your DeKalb Farmers Market
properties.description: World-famous international farmers market featuring global cuisines, fresh produce, and cultural diversity since 1977.
properties.address: 3000 E Ponce de Leon Ave, Decatur, GA 30030

properties.name: East Lake Golf Club
properties.description: Historic golf club, home course of Bobby Jones, and host of the PGA Tour Championship. Symbol of community revitalization.

properties.name: Morehouse College
properties.description: Prestigious HBCU and alma mater of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., producing leaders for over 150 years.

properties.name: Spelman College
properties.description: The nation's leading HBCU for women, consistently ranked among the best liberal arts colleges in America.
